apollo_data.description_seed:
Mintlify is an AI-native documentation platform founded in 2022 and based in San Francisco, California. The company aims to empower developers by simplifying the creation, management, and delivery of high-quality developer content. With a team of 28 employees, Mintlify serves over 10,000 companies and reaches 50 million developers annually. The company has received $21-22 million in funding from notable investors, including Andreessen Horowitz and Y Combinator.

The platform offers a range of features designed to enhance developer documentation, including guides, API references, and knowledge bases. It integrates AI to provide intelligent writing assistance, user feedback, and analytics. Mintlify also supports interactive features like personalized content and AI chat interfaces, making it easier for teams to manage their documentation. The company emphasizes quick deployment and offers enterprise services, including migration support and compliance with industry standards.

pages.title: Mintlify Company Overview
pages.description: An AI-native documentation platform powering intelligent knowledge infrastructure for developers and enterprises.

sections.content: Mintlify is an AI-native documentation platform that helps engineering teams build, maintain, and deliver intelligent knowledge infrastructure. Founded in 2022 in San Francisco, the company serves over 10,000 organizations — including Anthropic, Coinbase, PayPal, Fidelity, and HubSpot — reaching 50 million developers annually. Mintlify's platform combines AI-powered writing assistance, interactive API playgrounds, and an MCP server integration designed to keep AI agents accurate and up to date with current product documentation.
Scanned on July 11, 2025. Sources: Apollo enrichment, website crawl (12 pages), DNS records, TLS certificate, HTTP headers, trust center (Drata).

Mintlify maintains a Drata-powered trust center, a published responsible disclosure program, 13 named subprocessors, and documented legal policies. SOC 2 compliance is referenced through their trust center partnership with Drata.

key_customers.value: Anthropic, Coinbase, PayPal, Fidelity, HubSpot, Zapier, AT&T, Perplexity, X, Laravel, Lovable, Glean, Replit, Pinecone, Together AI

faqs.question: What does Mintlify do?
faqs.answer: Mintlify provides an AI-native documentation platform that enables companies to build, manage, and deliver developer-facing knowledge bases, API references, and interactive guides. The platform includes AI-powered search, a conversational assistant, and MCP server integration for agent-driven workflows.

faqs.question: Who are Mintlify's notable customers?
faqs.answer: Mintlify's published customer roster includes Anthropic, Coinbase, PayPal, Fidelity, HubSpot, Zapier, AT&T, Perplexity, X (Twitter), Laravel, Replit, Lovable, Glean, Pinecone, Ollama, and Together AI, among others.

faqs.question: How much funding has Mintlify raised?
faqs.answer: Mintlify has raised $21.3 million in total funding, with Andreessen Horowitz and Y Combinator among its investors.

pages.disclaimer: This profile was generated from publicly available information and has not been reviewed or verified by Mintlify. Information may be incomplete or outdated.

pages.title: Mintlify Security Overview
pages.description: Technical security posture and controls observed for Mintlify's documentation platform.

sections.content: Mintlify's website supports TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 with a valid certificate issued by Google Trust Services, expiring October 6, 2025. HSTS is enforced with a max-age of 63,072,000 seconds (approximately two years). All traffic is served over HTTPS through Vercel's edge network.

Mintlify runs on Vercel (Next.js hosting and edge delivery) with AWS infrastructure for backend services including Amazon EKS and ECS container orchestration. ClickHouse Cloud and MongoDB Atlas provide database services. Cloudflare handles DNS resolution. The platform maintains a published uptime record of 99.86% for documentation, 100% for updates, and 99.98% for the dashboard.

Mintlify uses Stytch as its authentication provider for signup and login flows. SSO login is available on Enterprise plans. The dashboard supports role-based access control (RBAC) and user permissions on Enterprise tier. Password protection is available starting at the Pro plan.

Mintlify publishes a comprehensive privacy policy (last updated January 16, 2026) which states the company does not process sensitive personal information and does not receive data from third parties. The privacy notice covers data subject rights for multiple jurisdictions. Mintlify's AI features use Anthropic and OpenAI as providers, with separate terms governing their data handling.
A full vendor risk assessment is available through DSALTA's platform. Claim this page to unlock.

faqs.question: Does Mintlify have a trust center?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mintlify operates a Drata-powered trust center at security.mintlify.com.

faqs.question: What authentication provider does Mintlify use?
faqs.answer: Mintlify uses Stytch as its authentication provider, managing signup and login flows and storing user emails.

pages.title: Mintlify Compliance Status
pages.description: Current compliance posture for Mintlify based on publicly available certifications and statements.

items.name: SOC 2
items.detail: Mintlify operates a Drata-powered trust center at security.mintlify.com. Drata is a SOC 2 compliance automation platform, and Mintlify's enterprise page references security review and compliance with industry standards. The trust center's presence through Drata is a strong indicator of SOC 2 engagement, but the specific Type I or Type II report was not publicly confirmed on the trust center page during this scan.

items.name: GDPR
items.detail: Mintlify's privacy policy (updated January 16, 2026) references data subject rights for EU/EEA residents and provides mechanisms for exercising privacy rights. The company's legal name is Mintlify, Inc. with a stated contact at hi@mintlify.com. No standalone DPA document was found at mintlify.com/legal/dpa.

items.name: ISO 27001
items.detail: No ISO 27001 certification was referenced on Mintlify's security page, trust center, enterprise page, or pricing page.

items.name: HIPAA
items.detail: No HIPAA compliance statement or BAA offering was found in Mintlify's published legal or security documentation.

faqs.question: Is Mintlify SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Mintlify maintains a Drata-powered trust center at security.mintlify.com, which is a SOC 2 compliance automation platform. The specific SOC 2 report type (Type I or Type II) was not publicly confirmed on the trust center during this scan.

faqs.question: Is Mintlify GDPR compliant?
faqs.answer: Mintlify's privacy policy references EU/EEA data subject rights and provides a contact for privacy inquiries. No standalone Data Processing Agreement was published at mintlify.com/legal/dpa.

pages.title: Mintlify Policies & Legal
pages.description: Published legal and policy documents available from Mintlify.

items.name: Privacy Policy
items.summary: Covers data collection, processing, sharing, and retention practices for Mintlify's services. States the company does not process sensitive personal information and does not receive information from third parties.

items.summary: Governs use of Mintlify's platform, covering account types (individual and enterprise), implementation assistance, support SLAs, licensing terms, and data protection obligations.

items.summary: Defines Mintlify's vulnerability reporting process, in-scope systems (mintlify.com/docs, dashboard.mintlify.com, leaves.mintlify.com, GitHub apps), out-of-scope activities, and report format requirements. Reports are sent to security@mintlify.com.

items.detail: Mintlify uses Anthropic as an AI/LLM provider. Anthropic's terms of use govern use of their generative AI services within Mintlify.
OpenAI is listed as an AI/LLM provider. OpenAI's terms of use and business terms apply to data processed through their services.
Google's generative AI terms of service apply to Google AI services used within Mintlify's platform.

pages.title: Mintlify Incident Response
pages.description: Incident response capabilities and monitoring observed for Mintlify.

items.item: Mintlify operates a public status page at status.mintlify.com with real-time operational status for three components: Documentation, Updates, and Dashboard.
The status page reports historical uptime: Documentation at 99.86%, Updates at 100%, and Dashboard at 99.98% for the December 2025 through March 2026 period.
Mintlify's pricing page confirms 24/7 incident monitoring is available on the Enterprise plan.

Mintlify maintains a responsible disclosure program at mintlify.com/security/responsible-disclosure, accepting vulnerability reports at security@mintlify.com with defined in-scope systems and a structured reporting format (CVSS scores required).
The responsible disclosure program defines in-scope targets: mintlify.com/docs, dashboard.mintlify.com, leaves.mintlify.com, and Mintlify GitHub apps.

pages.title: Mintlify Website Security Scan
pages.description: Technical security scan results for mintlify.com covering HTTP headers, TLS configuration, DNS email security, and cookie behavior.

cards.detail: HSTS is enforced with max-age=63072000 (approximately two years), ensuring browsers always connect over HTTPS.
Set to DENY, preventing mintlify.com from being embedded in iframes on any domain and mitigating clickjacking attacks.
No Content-Security-Policy header was detected on mintlify.com. A CSP helps prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by restricting which resources the browser can load.
No X-Content-Type-Options header was found. This header (typically set to 'nosniff') prevents browsers from MIME-type sniffing, reducing the risk of drive-by downloads.
No Permissions-Policy header was detected. This header controls which browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation) the site can access.
The Server header reveals 'Vercel' and X-Powered-By reveals 'Next.js'. While this confirms the hosting and framework stack, it also discloses technology information to potential attackers.

sections.heading: TLS / SSL Configuration
sections.detail: Mintlify supports TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. The certificate is valid, issued by Google Trust Services, and expires on October 6, 2025 (87 days remaining at scan time).

cards.detail: SPF record authorizes Google Workspace (_spf.google.com), SendGrid, and Stytch with a hard fail (-all) policy, preventing unauthorized servers from sending email on behalf of mintlify.com.
DKIM is configured for mintlify.com, enabling recipient mail servers to verify email message authenticity.

DMARC is configured with a 'reject' policy (p=reject), the strictest enforcement level. Aggregate reports are sent to dmarc@mintlify.com. This instructs receiving mail servers to reject unauthenticated emails claiming to be from mintlify.com.
Mail is handled by Google Workspace (aspmx.l.google.com and alternates).

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.detail: One cookie was set before any user consent interaction: a PostHog bootstrapData cookie containing feature flag configuration and a distinct ID. No cookie consent banner or mechanism was detected on the homepage.

faqs.question: Does mintlify.com enforce HTTPS?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mintlify enforces HTTPS via HSTS with a two-year max-age policy and supports TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3.

faqs.question: Is Mintlify's email domain protected against spoofing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mintlify has SPF (hard fail), DKIM, and DMARC (reject policy) configured — the strongest standard email authentication posture.

pages.disclaimer: This profile was generated from publicly available information and has not been reviewed or verified by Mintlify. Information may be incomplete or outdated.

pages.title: Mintlify Assessment Findings
pages.description: Summary of security posture findings for Mintlify derived from automated scanning and public documentation review.

cards.finding: Mintlify has a strong email security posture with SPF (hard fail), DKIM, and DMARC (reject policy) all configured. This is the most restrictive standard configuration available.
TLS 1.2 and 1.3 are supported with a valid certificate from Google Trust Services. HSTS is enforced with a two-year max-age. X-Frame-Options is set to DENY.

Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, and Permissions-Policy headers are absent from mintlify.com. While HSTS and X-Frame-Options are in place, the missing headers leave gaps in browser-side protections against XSS and MIME-sniffing.

Mintlify operates a responsible disclosure program with defined in-scope systems, a structured report format requiring CVSS scoring, and a dedicated security contact (security@mintlify.com). This demonstrates a mature approach to community-sourced vulnerability identification.

Mintlify publishes a complete subprocessor list with 13 services and 3 AI providers, each with linked privacy policies. This level of vendor transparency exceeds what most companies of Mintlify's size disclose publicly.

One cookie (PostHog bootstrapData) is set before any user consent interaction, and no cookie consent mechanism was detected. Organizations subject to GDPR or ePrivacy Directive requirements should evaluate this against their compliance needs.
Unlock detailed compliance assessment by claiming this page on DSALTA.
Unlock penetration testing assessment by claiming this page on DSALTA.

faqs.question: What are the main security gaps identified in Mintlify's public posture?
faqs.answer: The primary gaps are missing HTTP security headers (Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, Permissions-Policy) and a pre-consent cookie being set without a visible consent mechanism. Email security and transport encryption are strong.

pages.title: Mintlify Security FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked security, compliance, and procurement questions about Mintlify.

faqs.question: Is Mintlify SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Mintlify operates a Drata-powered trust center at security.mintlify.com. Drata is a SOC 2 compliance automation platform, and the presence of this trust center is consistent with active SOC 2 compliance efforts. The specific report type was not publicly confirmed during this scan.

faqs.question: Has Mintlify had any data breaches or security incidents?
faqs.answer: No public security incidents or data breaches involving Mintlify were identified in this scan. Mintlify's status page at status.mintlify.com shows operational history but no security incident disclosures. Gemini search was unavailable during this scan, so news database coverage could not be confirmed.

faqs.question: Does Mintlify use customer data for AI model training?
faqs.answer: Mintlify's privacy policy and third-party provider terms do not contain an explicit statement addressing whether customer documentation content is used for AI model training by Mintlify or its LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google).

faqs.question: Does Mintlify support SSO?
faqs.answer: Yes. SSO login is listed as a feature on Mintlify's Enterprise plan. The underlying authentication infrastructure uses Stytch.

faqs.question: Is Mintlify GDPR compliant?
faqs.answer: Mintlify's privacy policy references EU/EEA data subject rights including access, correction, and deletion. No standalone Data Processing Agreement was found at mintlify.com/legal/dpa.

faqs.question: Does Mintlify have a bug bounty or responsible disclosure program?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mintlify publishes a responsible disclosure policy at mintlify.com/security/responsible-disclosure. Vulnerability reports are accepted at security@mintlify.com with defined in-scope systems and a requirement for CVSS-scored reports. Bounties may be issued at Mintlify's discretion.

faqs.question: Where is Mintlify's customer data stored?
faqs.answer: Mintlify uses AWS, ClickHouse Cloud, MongoDB Atlas, and Vercel for infrastructure and data storage. Specific geographic regions and data residency options were not disclosed in Mintlify's public documentation.

faqs.question: Does Mintlify perform penetration testing?
faqs.answer: No explicit statement about penetration testing was found in Mintlify's public security documentation or trust center page. Mintlify's Drata trust center and responsible disclosure program demonstrate active security practices, but specific pen testing disclosures were not identified.

faqs.question: What is Mintlify's uptime SLA?
faqs.answer: Mintlify advertises a 99.99% uptime SLA on its Enterprise plan. Custom SLAs are available for Enterprise customers. The public status page shows recent uptime of 99.86% for Documentation, 100% for Updates, and 99.98% for the Dashboard.

faqs.question: Who are Mintlify's investors?
faqs.answer: Mintlify has raised $21.3 million in total funding from Andreessen Horowitz and Y Combinator.

faqs.question: Where is Mintlify headquartered?
faqs.answer: Mintlify is headquartered in San Francisco, California, United States.

faqs.question: Who are Mintlify's major customers?
faqs.answer: Mintlify's published customer page lists Anthropic, Coinbase, PayPal, Fidelity, HubSpot, Zapier, AT&T, Perplexity, X (Twitter), Laravel, Replit, Lovable, Glean, Pinecone, Ollama, Together AI, Anaconda, and others.
